Q13 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding the decision process using the Chi-square test?
S1 : If the calculated p-value is less than the significance level, the null hypothesis (H0) is rejected.
S2 : Rejecting the null hypothesis means there is sufficient evidence to conclude that an association exists between the variables.
S3 : A high χ2 value always leads to a rejection of the null hypothesis, regardless of the degrees of freedom.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• The critical χ2 value needed for rejection is dependent on the degrees of freedom; a high
value in one scenario may not be high enough in another. So, S3 is incorrect.

Q17 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are correct?

Correct answer: A, B, D
Solution

(a, b, d)
• As dimensionality increases, distances between points become less meaningful, making KNN less effective. So, option (a) is correct.
• Unlike K-Means (which uses centroids that may not correspond to actual points), K-Medoids
selects actual data points as cluster centers, which improves interpretability. So, option (d) is correct.
• K-Means only guarantees convergence to a local minimum, not the global minimum. The final
solution depends on initial centroid selection. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• Hierarchical clustering produces a dendrogram. The number of clusters can be chosen after
clustering by cutting the dendrogram at the desired level. So, option (d) is correct.

Q18 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following inference algorithms for a Bayesian network is classified as an exact method that guarantees a precise calculation of the conditional probability?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Variable Elimination systematically computes the required probability by summing out all nonquery variables using factor operations.
Since it performs exact marginalization without sampling, it guarantees a precise conditional probability.

Q21 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0
Not attempted

T(n – 1) + Ο(n)
Which of the following standard sorting algorithms has a tight worst-case complexity that directly corresponds to this recurrence?

Correct answer: B, C
Solution

(b, c)
• Quick Sort’s worst-case recurrence is also T(n) = T(n – 1) + Ο(n), leading to Ο(n2). Since Bubble
Sort’s worst-case is consistently Ο(n2), it is the most direct fit from the options.
• Bubble Sort’s passes involve Ο(n), Ο(n – 1), ... comparisons. This is better described by
T(n) = T(n – 1) + Ο(n) which sums to Ο(n2).

Q22 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are correct?

Correct answer: B, C
Solution

(b, c)
• Star schema is denormalized, not normalized. It intentionally keeps redundancy to speed up
query performance. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• Snowflake schema applies full normalization to dimension tables. This removes redundancy
and breaks dimensions into multiple related tables. So, option (b) is correct.
• Normalization in snowflake schema reduces repeated dimension attributes. Hence, it provides
better storage efficiency. So, option (c) is correct.
• Star schema uses denormalized tables, which increase memory usage. Its advantage is faster
query performance, not lower memory consumption. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q31 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the choice of k in k-fold cross-validation. Which of the following statements is/are correct?
S1 : Choosing a smaller k generally results in a lower bias for the test error estimate.
S2 : Choosing a smaller k means a smaller proportion of data is used for training, increasing the potential for high variance in the final model’s performance.
S3 : The standard choices of k = 5 or k = 10 are used because they are typically seen as the best
balance between reducing bias and minimizing computation time.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Choosing a smaller k (eg. k = 3) means each training fold has less data, so the model sees fewer examples. This leads to higher bias in the test error estimate because the model is less representative of the full dataset. Therefore, smaller k does not lower bias, it actually increases it. So, S1 is incorrect.
• Smaller k reduces the amount of data used for training in each fold. As a result, the model can vary more depending on which fold is left out, causing higher variance in performance. This makes the final error estimate less stable across different folds. So, S2 is correct.
• Standard choices of k = 5 or k = 10 strike a balance between computational cost and accuracy of the error estimate. Larger k reduces bias but increases computation, smaller k reduces computation but increases variance. Hence, k = 5 or 10 is commonly used as a practical compromise. So, S3 is correct.

Q37 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statement:
S1: The probability of the Type-I error cannot be higher than the probability of the Type-II error.
S2 : Type-II error occurs if the test accepts the null hypothesis when the null hypothesis is actually false.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• There is no restriction that α ≤ β.
Depending on the test design, Type-I error (α) can be smaller, equal, or larger than Type-II error (β). So, S1 is incorrect.
• This is the definition of Type-Il error: Fail to reject H0 even though it is false. So, S2 is correct.

Q38 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding the computational cost of crossvalidation methods?
S1 : For linear regression, LOOCV can be performed with the cost of a single model fit using a specific computational shortcut.
S2 : For most complex non-linear models on large datasets, LOOCV is computationally prohibitive, making k-fold cross validation the practical choice.
S3 : The model chosen via k-fold cross validation’s error estimate is usually retrained on the entire dataset to create the final production model.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• For linear regression, LOOCV can use a hat matrix shortcut, avoiding n separate model fits.
This reduces computational cost to roughly a single model fit. So, S1 is correct.
• For complex non-linear models or large datasets, LOOCV requires training n times, which is
very expensive. Using k-fold CV reduces computation while still providing a reliable error
estimate. So, S2 is correct.
• After k-fold CV is used to estimate errors or select hyperparameters, the model is usually
retrained on the full dataset. This ensures the final production model uses all available data,
improving performance. So, S3 is correct.

Q41 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

In α – β pruning, a MIN node has an inherited α value of 15 and its own β value is currently 18.
If it finds a child with utility 16, what happens next?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
At MIN node, the value is update as β = min (current β, child value).
The MIN node update β to min (18, 16) = 16.
Pruning occurring only if a α ≥ β.
Here, 15 > 16 (false).
So, pruning therefore, the MIN node update β to 16 and continue evaluating other children.

Q43 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1: Depth-Limited Search (DLS) uses less space than Breadth-First Search (BFS).
S2 : Breadth-First Search (BFS) has a space complexity of Ο(bd), as it stores all nodes at each level until the goal is found.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• DLS, like DFS, only needs to store nodes along a single path of depth l, giving a space complexity
of Ο(l). BFS must store all nodes at the current frontier, which grows exponentially. Therefore,
DLS uses far less space than BFS. So, S1 is correct.
• BFS expands level by level and keeps every generated node in memory. With branching
factor b and solution depth d, storing all nodes leads to Ο(bd). So, S2 is also correct.

Q52 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct about Principal Component Analysis (PCA)?

Correct answer: A, B
Solution

(a, b)
• PCA creates new features called principal components, which are linear combinations of the
original features. These components are orthogonal to each other, making PCA a feature
extraction technique. So, option (a) is correct.
• The first principal component captures the maximum variance present in the data. It represents
the direction along which the data is most spread out. So, option (b) is correct.
• PCA does not use class labels, so it cannot maximize class separation. It is an unsupervised
technique, unlike LDA which is supervised. So, option (c) is incorrect.

Q61 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct about the Single Linkage method in Hierarchical
Clustering?
S1 : The distance between two clusters is determined by the minimum distance between any observation in the first cluster and any observation in the second cluster.
S2 : Single Linkage is known to suffer from the ‘chaining’ effect, where distinct clusters are merged due to a single close pair of points on their boundaries
S3 : Single Linkage is also referred to as the Maximum Linkage method.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Single Linkage uses the minimum distance. So, S1 is correct.
• This method is prone to chaining, leading to elongated, less compact clusters. So, S2 is correct.
